instructions: Read each passage carefully and answer the questions. Answers must be based only on the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Richard Ebright showed an interest in science from a very young age. As a child, he was curious about the natural world and spent much of his time observing insects. His early fascination with butterflies later developed into a serious scientific pursuit.
questions: Who is mentioned in the passage?
What interested Richard Ebright during childhood?
Which natural activity did he spend time on?
Which insect is specifically mentioned?
How did his childhood interest develop later?

passages.paragraph: Richard Ebright’s mother played a crucial role in his development. She encouraged his curiosity and provided him with books and tools to explore science. Her constant support helped him grow intellectually.
questions: Who supported Richard Ebright’s learning?
What role did his mother play?
What did she provide to encourage his learning?
How did her support help him?
Which quality of Ebright is highlighted here?

passages.paragraph: One book that deeply influenced Ebright was ‘The Travels of Monarch X’. This book introduced him to real scientific research and inspired him to study butterfly migration seriously.
questions: Which book is mentioned in the passage?
How did the book influence Ebright?
What scientific topic did it introduce?
What did the book inspire him to study?
What type of research is referred to?

passages.paragraph: Ebright participated in a science fair but did not win. His project was only a display and lacked experimentation. This failure made him realise the true nature of scientific work.
questions: What event did Ebright participate in?
Did he win the event?
What was lacking in his project?
What did this failure teach him?
How is scientific work described here?

passages.paragraph: After his failure, Ebright changed his approach to science. He began performing real experiments instead of preparing simple models. This shift marked an important turning point in his learning.
questions: What happened after Ebright’s failure?
What change did he make in his approach?
What did he stop doing?
What did he begin doing instead?
Why is this change important?

passages.paragraph: Ebright conducted experiments on monarch butterflies. He focused on their growth and development and made important observations that contributed to scientific knowledge.
questions: Which insect did Ebright experiment on?
What was the focus of his experiments?
What did he study about the insect?
What did his observations contribute to?
What type of work is described here?

passages.paragraph: Through careful observation, Ebright discovered that the gold spots on monarch pupae produce hormones. These hormones are necessary for the butterfly’s growth.
questions: What discovery is mentioned in the passage?
Where are the gold spots found?
What do the gold spots produce?
Why are these hormones important?
Which stage of the butterfly is discussed?

passages.paragraph: Dr Frederick A. Urquhart guided Ebright by suggesting meaningful experiments. His guidance helped Ebright develop scientific thinking and discipline.
questions: Who guided Richard Ebright?
What kind of help did he provide?
How did the guidance help Ebright?
Which skills did Ebright develop?
What role does guidance play here?

passages.paragraph: The lesson shows that curiosity, perseverance, and hard work are essential qualities of a scientist. Ebright’s journey proves that success is achieved through continuous effort.
questions: Which qualities are mentioned in the passage?
Who demonstrates these qualities?
What does Ebright’s journey prove?
How is success described here?
What is necessary to achieve success?

passages.paragraph: ‘The Making of a Scientist’ highlights inquiry-based learning. It shows that learning through experimentation is more effective than memorisation.
questions: What type of learning is highlighted?
What method of learning is preferred?
What is considered less effective?
What does the lesson emphasise?
How is learning best achieved according to the passage?
